releases.title: Smoother, faster releases — every update now lands on your Mac automatically.

highlights.title: We can ship updates to you faster now
highlights.body: We rebuilt our release process so every new version goes through the exact same steps — no missed details, no chance of a broken update reaching you. That means you get improvements sooner and with more confidence.

highlights.title: Safer by default
highlights.body: If anything is out of place when we're preparing a release — even something small — Pier refuses to ship it. Nothing half-baked lands on your Mac.

releases.title: First update delivered automatically — no download needed on your end.

highlights.title: Arrived without a download
highlights.body: If you were using Pier 1.1.0, this version landed on your Mac on its own — no download link to click, no installer to drag. Every release from here on works the same way.

highlights.title: A small rename behind the scenes
highlights.body: A few leftover "FTP Manager" references from early development got renamed to "Pier." You'll only notice if you check technical logs — but it's cleaner.

releases.title: A more secure way to connect, and updates that arrive on their own.

highlights.title: Connect securely with SFTP
highlights.body: If your hosting provider offers the modern secure connection (called "SFTP"), Pier now speaks it. Pick it from the dropdown when you add a new site — Pier fills in the right settings automatically.

highlights.title: Updates arrive on their own
highlights.body: Pier now checks for new versions once a day in the background. When one is ready, it downloads quietly and installs the next time you quit — no manual download, no disruption.

highlights.title: Licence keys pasted from email "just work"
highlights.body: Some email apps break long licence keys across two lines when you copy them. Pier now handles that gracefully — just paste and activate.

highlights.title: The app finally says "Pier" everywhere
highlights.body: A few menus still called the app by its working name from early development. They now say Pier, like you'd expect.

highlights.title: Better at telling the difference between a typo and a broken network
highlights.body: If you mistype your password, Pier says so clearly — instead of backing off like it would for a flaky internet connection. Fewer false "too many attempts" moments.

releases.title: Pier is live. Your website, without the hassle.

highlights.title: The first version
highlights.body: Everything Pier does today started here — a native Mac app for anyone who manages a website but would rather not open a code editor to change a phone number.

highlights.title: Connect to any website
highlights.body: Whether your site runs WordPress, Joomla, Drupal, a webshop, or something hand-built — if your hosting gave you the standard connection details, Pier can work with it.

highlights.title: Talk to Pier, it does the work
highlights.body: Instead of hunting through folders, you type what you want ("change the phone number everywhere it appears"). Pier finds the right files, shows you exactly what it plans to change, and waits for your OK.

highlights.title: Safe by design
highlights.body: Every change is a before/after preview you approve. If something looks wrong afterwards, one click puts it back. Every file Pier touches gets automatically backed up to your Mac.

highlights.title: Built-in site checks
highlights.body: Search-engine visibility, security leaks, loading speed, broken links, mobile display — Pier can scan your site for any of these on request, and offer to fix what it finds.

highlights.title: Edit your shop's products and orders too
highlights.body: If your site uses a database (most do — WordPress, webshops), Pier can look things up and carefully change single items with your approval. Perfect for "mark this product out of stock."

highlights.title: Free 30-page guide, English and Dutch
highlights.body: A friendly walkthrough of everything Pier can do, written for people who don't code. Linked at the top of the download page.
